title: True Line
desc: The only way to beat a strong mirage is to give the eye honest reference points before the desert starts inventing better ones.

steps.text:
By midmorning, Mipedim Mirage starts improving on reality. The flats stretch into false avenues. Stone shelves lift into floating ledges. A bare distance becomes a whole convincing structure for the length of a breath and then melts back into heat before anyone can blame themselves properly. That is why the survey posts carry mirrors and cord grids. If the eye insists on believing whatever arrives most dramatically, the field crews answer with measured geometry.

The sight master checks a grid frame against the far ridge and frowns when the cords refuse to line up. "Polish the reference mirrors if you want the careful work," he says. "Or retension the cord grid between the survey posts if you would rather correct the frame itself. Either way, the truth will need help in about ten minutes."

options.label: Polish the reference mirrors and clear the sight marks
Retension the cord grid between the survey posts

steps.text:
The mirrors cloud quickly out here. Fine dust settles, breath traces when someone leans too close, and the smallest smear turns a fixed point into another uncertain glow on the horizon. You polish each plate until the etched cross marks appear sharp again, then test the reflection against the ridge stones the crews use as their daily constants. Once clean, the mirrors stop flattering the mirage and begin undoing it. False walls collapse. Phantom roads break into heat bands. Distance regains its proper reluctance.

The sight master looks through the restored frame and grunts approval. It is a modest sound, but in this field it means the world has briefly consented to behave. "Good," he says. "The desert may improvise. We will continue taking notes."

options.label: Cover the cleaned mirrors and leave the marks sharp

steps.text:
The cord grid spans between four low posts and turns empty air into a measured plane the eye can compare against itself. Right now the lower line sags, which is enough to make the far heat shimmer look taller than it is and closer than anyone should trust. You tighten each span in turn, reset the corner knots, and pluck the finished lines until they answer with the same taut note from post to post. Only then do you sight through the frame again.

The difference is immediate. The supposed doorway on the flats buckles into nothing. A second false ridge sinks back where it belongs. The sight master nods once, more to the geometry than to you. "There. A proper frame. If illusion wants to be convincing, let it first explain itself to the grid."

options.label: Coil the spare cord and leave the frame honest again
